sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 52-year-old male, presents with a history of injuring his right knee while trampolining a week ago, reporting a "snap" and immediate pain.
He has been experiencing pain on the lateral aspect of the knee, with some bruising and swelling.
He has been trying to manage the pain with Tylenol, but it has not provided significant relief.
He has a history of hypertension and diabetes, and is currently taking 20mg of lisinopril and metformin.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes, for which he is taking 20mg of lisinopril and metformin.
He has not been adherent to his medication regimen, and his blood pressure has been elevated at 180/80.
He has also not been consistent with his exercise regimen, but has been trying to improve his diet.
Review of systems: The patient reports that his blood sugars have been a bit better, but he has not been checking them regularly, and has been trying to watch his carb intake.
He does not have a specific diet, but is trying to eat what he feels like.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 180/80, which is elevated.
Physical exam: The patient has a slight limp and ecchymosis and edema on the lateral aspect of the knee, but no effusion.
He has a decent range of motion, but experiences pain with movement.
The heart has a regular rate and rhythm, and lungs are clear bilaterally.
Test Results: The patient's right knee x-ray shows no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Right knee pain; hypertension; diabetes; suspected kidney disease (status: confirmed); suspected cardiovascular disease (status: unknown); suspected renal disease (status: unknown)
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling in the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20mg daily for hypertension, and order an echo to assess cardiac function.
I will also order an A1c to assess long-term blood sugar control, and a lipid panel to assess lipid levels.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the tests and to reassess the knee injury.
